scores.user_input: What is the policy for delayed shipments?
retrieved_contexts:
2. Geographic Exclusions and Carrier-Specific Limitations
International transit corridors to Eastern Europe and Central Asia are subject to strict customs verification. If a shipment is seized or delayed by foreign customs due to suspected regulatory non-compliance of the imported goods, the transit insurance policy is immediately suspended. It is the importer's responsibility to verify that the items ordered are compliant with local import restrictions. Furthermore, customs clearance fees, import duties, and VAT are not covered by this policy and must be paid directly to the local taxing authority in accordance with doc_07_customs_duties.pdf.
Carrier-specific limitations apply to remote areas. For example, DHL Express does not support signature-required delivery in certain remote islands. In these cases, if the customer requests signature delivery, VoltVibe will attempt to reroute the package via FedEx. If no signature-capable carrier is available for the destination, the transaction will be canceled and a full refund issued.

3.1 Carrier Hold Windows & Refusal Abandonment
Packages delayed at local customs checkpoints are typically held for a maximum of 14 calendar days. The carrier will make three attempts to contact the importer using the phone number and email address provided at checkout. If the customer refuses to pay the assessed import fees, or fails to respond within the 14-day hold window, the shipment is classified as refused. If a package is refused, the carrier will charge VoltVibe return shipping freight costs. REFUSAL PENALTY: VoltVibe will deduct the carrier's return shipping costs, import duties, and brokerage fees from the customer's final product refund. If the return shipping costs exceed the value of the order, the package is declared abandoned at customs, resulting in a complete loss of the shipment. In this scenario, the customer receives zero refund or store credit.

4.2 International Battery Customs Audits
Certain countries, particularly Australia and Germany, conduct physical compliance audits on Class 9 Dangerous Goods. These audits can delay packages by 5 to 10 additional business days at the port of entry. VoltVibe is not liable for transit timeline failures resulting from regulatory battery audits, and shipping charges will not be refunded for shipments affected by HAZMAT-related custom delays.

1. Scope of Coverage & Carrier Logistics
Opting out of the protection program relieves VoltVibe of all liability for loss or damage during transit, and the buyer assumes all risks of transit. For orders exceeding $1,000 USD, enrollment in Premium Signature Protection is mandatory and cannot be waived, as detailed in Section 4.2. Our logistics team monitors all carrier transit corridors to identify potential bottlenecks. If a package is rerouted due to natural disasters, labor disputes, or transportation strikes, the active transit cycle is extended automatically to cover the additional transit duration. However, this extension does not apply to shipments that are delayed at international borders due to customer documentation failures. VoltVibe reserves the right to select the primary carrier for all standard shipping orders. If a customer requests a specific carrier at checkout, any additional shipping surcharges will be billed directly to the
customer and are non-refundable, even if a transit claim is subsequently approved.

2. Geographic Exclusions and Carrier-Specific Limitations
For shipments destined for US military bases (APO/FPO/DPO), transit insurance terminates the moment the package is handed over to the Military Postal Service Agency (MPSA). Any subsequent loss, theft, or damage occurring within the military postal network is outside the scope of this policy, and claims must be filed directly with the MPSA.

*   **International transit corridors to Eastern Europe and Central Asia:** If a shipment is seized or delayed by foreign customs due to suspected regulatory non-compliance of the imported goods, the transit insurance policy is immediately suspended (Document 1).

*   **Customer documentation failures:** Transit cycle extensions due to rerouting due to natural disasters, labor disputes, or transportation strikes do not apply to shipments delayed at international borders due to customer documentation failures (Document 4).

6.2 Point Expiration Provisions
All loyalty program points, regardless of user membership tier, are subject to a hard expiration date on December 31st of the second calendar year following the date the points were credited to the account. For example, points earned on March 15, 2026, will expire on December 31, 2028. Expired points are permanently deleted from the customer's account and cannot be restored. It is the customer's responsibility to monitor their point balances and redeem points before they expire.

THE EXPRIATION CONFLICT: Section 3 (Tier Retention) states that loyalty points earned under the Gold and Platinum membership tiers are considered permanent and do not expire as long as the member maintains their active tier status. In the event of a system dispute, the company will enforce the hard expiration date of Section 6.2, and any expired points will not be re-credited to the account.
To assist members in tracking their point balances, the customer dashboard displays a detailed points ledger. This ledger lists the acquisition date, point source (order number), and the specific projected expiration date for each point block. VoltVibe will send automated email reminders 60 days and 30 days prior to any point expiration events. It is the customer's responsibility to ensure that their notification preferences permit these emails.

The VoltVibe Loyalty Points Program is designed to reward our registered customers for purchases made directly through our online store. Members of the loyalty program earn points on all qualifying purchases. For every $1.00 USD spent on products (excluding taxes, shipping fees, and purchases made with gift cards or store credits), the member will earn 1 loyalty point. Points are credited to the member's account once the order
Loyalty points can be redeemed during checkout for discounts on future VoltVibe purchases, subject to redemption tiers and product exclusions defined in the account dashboard. All loyalty points earned in a member's account expire exactly 24 months from the date they were earned and credited. It is the responsibility
has successfully shipped. of the member to track point balances and use them before expiration.

Effective Date: January 1, 2026 cannot be combined across accounts.

3. Points Expiration and Tier Retention
To maintain Gold or Platinum VIP status, the customer's spend total must remain above the tier threshold on a rolling 12-month basis. If a customer's spending drops below the threshold, they will be downgraded to the appropriate tier at the end of the calendar month.

2. Membership Tiers and Point Multipliers
The VIP Club features three membership tiers based on cumulative purchases over a rolling 12-month period. Each tier unlocks a distinct points multiplier and exclusive benefits: 2.1 Silver Tier (Spend Threshold: $500 - $1,499) Silver members earn a 1x points multiplier, yielding 1 loyalty point per $1.00 USD spent (as defined in doc_02_loyalty_points.pdf). Silver tier benefits include access to member-only sales events, a $10 birthday promo coupon (valid for 30 days), and priority standard shipping processing. 2.2 Gold Tier (Spend Threshold: $1,500 - $4,999) Gold members earn a 1.5x points multiplier, yielding 1.5 loyalty points per $1.00 USD spent. Gold tier benefits include all Silver benefits, a $25 birthday promo coupon, free standard shipping on all orders, and early access to new product releases. For shipments to student dormitories or university campus mailrooms, delivery is considered complete once the parcel is signed for by campus mail staff. VoltVibe is not liable for internal university distribution delays or losses.

Grade A devices include a brand-new official OEM charger block and a certified charging cable (packaged inside a VoltVibe white retail box). Grade B devices include high-quality, UL-certified third-party charging accessories packaged in a generic brown cardboard box. Grade C devices do not include any accessories, charging blocks, or cables; they are shipped as 'device-only' units inside bubble-wrap protective bags. This accessory exclusion allows Grade C items to be sold at a significant discount. Accessories can be purchased separately at a 15% discount when ordered alongside a Grade C device, as detailed in our accessory product catalogue.

scores.user_input: What happens if my package is lost in transit?
retrieved_contexts:
4. Claim Filing Windows and Requirements
To ensure proper investigation and verification, claims must be filed within strict reporting windows. Filing windows depend on the category of the claim (Loss in Transit, Damaged Package, or Stolen Package). Failure to submit a claim within the designated period will result in immediate and final rejection of the claim without exception. All claims must be accompanied by the standard documentation package, which includes: the original order confirmation number, carrier tracking details, a signed declaration of non-receipt (for lost/stolen packages), and photographic evidence (for damaged packages).
A package is legally defined as 'Lost in Transit' when carrier tracking records show no scan activity for a prolonged period. The timeline for filing a Lost in Transit claim is conditional on the shipping tier selected at checkout:
· Standard Economy Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 14 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 30 calendar days from the date of shipment.
· Express and Priority Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 7 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 21 calendar days from the date of shipment.
· International Priority Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 21 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 45 calendar days from the date of shipment.

2. Geographic Exclusions and Carrier-Specific Limitations
For shipments destined for US military bases (APO/FPO/DPO), transit insurance terminates the moment the package is handed over to the Military Postal Service Agency (MPSA). Any subsequent loss, theft, or damage occurring within the military postal network is outside the scope of this policy, and claims must be filed directly with the MPSA.

1. Scope of Coverage & Carrier Logistics
Opting out of the protection program relieves VoltVibe of all liability for loss or damage during transit, and the buyer assumes all risks of transit. For orders exceeding $1,000 USD, enrollment in Premium Signature Protection is mandatory and cannot be waived, as detailed in Section 4.2. Our logistics team monitors all carrier transit corridors to identify potential bottlenecks. If a package is rerouted due to natural disasters, labor disputes, or transportation strikes, the active transit cycle is extended automatically to cover the additional transit duration. However, this extension does not apply to shipments that are delayed at international borders due to customer documentation failures. VoltVibe reserves the right to select the primary carrier for all standard shipping orders. If a customer requests a specific carrier at checkout, any additional shipping surcharges will be billed directly to the
customer and are non-refundable, even if a transit claim is subsequently approved.

4. Step-by-Step Recycling Preparation Guide
We recommend taking a photograph of the packaged battery inside the shipping box before sealing it. This photograph serves as vital documentation in the event that the carrier damages the package during transit, allowing our team to file a claim under the transit insurance policy (governed by doc_21).

3. Domestic vs. International Processing Pathways
Domestic claims processing requires the customer to submit a signed Declaration of Non-Receipt. This document is a legally binding statement verifying that the package was not received. Once submitted, our claims team will review carrier GPS logs to verify the exact coordinates of the delivery vehicle at the time of the marked delivery. If the GPS coordinates match the customer's delivery address within a 15-meter tolerance, the claim will be flagged for fraud investigation, and a physical inspection of the property may be requested.
International claims are often delayed due to time zone differences and varying carrier processing speeds. VoltVibe will provide weekly updates to the customer via email regarding the status of the carrier's investigation. If the carrier confirms the loss of the package before the end of the 30-day monitoring period, VoltVibe will expedite the replacement shipment. However, if the carrier reports that the package is still in transit, no replacement will be authorized until the 30-day window has fully expired.
In the event of a disputed delivery in Canada, the claim must be co-signed by the recipient and a witness if the total order value exceeds $250 CAD. This requirement is in place to comply with Canadian insurance underwriting regulations.
scores.response:
If your package is lost in transit, here is what you should know regarding claims:

1.  **Claim Filing Requirements:** Claims must be filed within strict reporting windows depending on the shipping tier selected at checkout (Document 1).
    *   **Standard Economy Shipping:** Claims can only be filed after 14 calendar days of zero scan updates and no later than 30 calendar days from the date of shipment (Document 1).
    *   **Express and Priority Shipping:** Claims can only be filed after 7 calendar days of zero scan updates and no later than 21 calendar days from the date of shipment (Document 1).
    *   **International Priority Shipping:** Claims can only be filed after 21 calendar days of zero scan updates and no later than 45 calendar days from the date of shipment (Document 1).
2.  **Documentation:** All claims must be accompanied by the standard documentation package, which includes the original order confirmation number, carrier tracking details, a signed declaration of non-receipt (for lost/stolen packages), and photographic evidence (for damaged packages) (Document 1).
3.  **Domestic Claims:** Domestic claims processing requires the customer to submit a signed Declaration of Non-Receipt. The claims team will then review carrier GPS logs to verify the delivery vehicle's coordinates. If the GPS coordinates match the delivery address within a 15-meter tolerance, the claim may be flagged for fraud investigation, and a physical inspection might be requested (Document 3).
4.  **International Claims:** VoltVibe will provide weekly updates regarding the carrier's investigation status. If the carrier confirms the loss before the end of the 30-day monitoring period, VoltVibe will expedite a replacement shipment. However, if the carrier reports the package is still in transit, no replacement will be authorized until the 30-day window has fully expired (Document 5).
5.  **Specific Carrier Situations:**
    *   For shipments to US military bases (APO/FPO/DPO), transit insurance terminates when the package is handed over to the Military Postal Service Agency (MPSA), and claims must be filed directly with the MPSA (Document 2).
    *   If a package is damaged, taking a photograph of the packaged battery inside the shipping box before sealing it can serve as documentation to file a claim under the transit insurance policy (governed by doc\_21) (Document 4).
scores.reference: A package is legally defined as 'Lost in Transit' when carrier tracking records show no scan activity for a prolonged period. Claims must be filed within strict reporting windows based on the shipping tier selected at checkout. Failure to submit a claim within the designated period will result in immediate and final rejection of the claim without exception.

scores.user_input: How do I claim a warranty repair?
retrieved_contexts:
Effective Date: January 1, 2026
All new VoltVibe branded consumer electronics hardware purchased directly from our website or authorized retail channels are covered by a standard 12-month limited manufacturer warranty. This coverage begins on the date of purchase or the date of delivery, whichever occurs later. Under this policy, VoltVibe guarantees that the hardware product will be free from material defects in workmanship and materials under normal, standard
consumer use. If a hardware defect arises and a valid claim is received within the 12-month warranty period, VoltVibe will, at its option, either repair the hardware defect at no charge using new or refurbished replacement parts, or replace the product with a new or refurbished unit of equal or greater value. This limited warranty does not cover damage caused by accident, abuse, misuse, liquid contact,
Exclusions: fire, earthquake, or other external causes. Specifically, water ingress, liquid damage, and drops or physical impacts resulting in cracked screens, casing breakage, or internal structural degradation are strictly excluded from coverage. Alterations, hardware modification, or unauthorized self-repairs also void this warranty immediately.

5.3 Regional Warranty Exceptions & Legal Rights
The terms outlined in this policy do not restrict or replace statutory consumer protection guarantees. Specifically, customers residing in the State of California are entitled to an implied warranty of merchantability for refurbished goods extending up to 1 year, and may request repair or replacement at authorized regional service hubs. EEA residents are covered by statutory guarantees matching local country laws. Customers must provide proof of primary residence to activate regional policy overrides.

5.1 Scope of the 90-Day Limited Warranty
The refurbished warranty covers mechanical and electrical component failures, such as touchscreen failure, malfunctioning ports, defective logical boards, or failing speaker elements. The warranty does *not* cover: cosmetic wear occurring after delivery, liquid damage (regardless of original factory rating, since seals degrade over time), accidental drop damage, or software modifications. Furthermore, battery degradation is excluded from warranty coverage: if a device's battery capacity drops below 80% *after* the purchase date, this is considered standard physical wear-and-tear unless the drop occurs within the first 14 calendar days of delivery, in which case it is treated as a defect present at the time of grading.

4. Claim Filing Windows and Requirements
For packages marked as 'delivered' by the carrier but arrived damaged, the customer must submit a claim along with photographic evidence within 10 business days of delivery. Deductions are cumulative, up to a maximum cap of 50% of the base valuation. This policy is necessary to account for the sudden decline in secondary market value when new product generations are introduced.

1. Program Overview & Eligibility Requirements
The VoltVibe Smartphone Trade-In Program is designed to enable registered customers to leverage their older smartphone hardware to acquire credit toward purchasing new VoltVibe products. To qualify for any trade-in credit valuation, devices must meet strict baseline eligibility requirements. The device must: power on, boot completely to the primary operating system, operate stably on battery power without being plugged into a continuous external power source, possess a fully functional display digitizer, and have zero cracks on either the display assembly or the rear protective casing glass. CRITICAL SHIPPING EXCLUSION: It is the customer's absolute responsibility to utilize the protective foam inserts included in the VoltVibe shipping kit. scores.user_input: What are the Freight Shipping Requirements (LTL Cargo) for shipments exceeding 70 lbs like the VoltVibe Titan PowerStation?
retrieved_contexts:
4. Freight Shipping Requirements (LTL Cargo)
Large items or heavy bulk orders exceeding a physical scale weight of 70 lbs (such as multi-unit solar array bundles or dual VoltVibe Titan PowerStation setups) cannot be shipped using standard delivery carriers.

4.1 Heavy LTL Freight Regulations & Delivery Surcharges
CRITICAL CARGO RULE: Heavy cargo exceeding 70 lbs must be shipped via Less-Than-Truckload (LTL) freight carriers. LTL freight shipments are ineligible for Express or Overnight shipping options. Freight transit times range between 5 to 10 business days within the contiguous United States. Furthermore, LTL delivery to a residential address requires a scheduling appointment with the carrier and lift-gate truck service, which incurs a mandatory $75.00 LTL residential freight surcharge. This charge is automatically added to the shipping total at checkout and cannot be waived. If the customer fails to be present during their scheduled LTL delivery appointment window, the carrier will charge a $50.00 redelivery fee, which must be paid before the carrier will attempt a second delivery.

4. HAZMAT Shipping and Regulatory Audits
The shipment of high-capacity lithium-ion battery modules introduces significant customs compliance challenges. Products containing battery modules exceeding 100 Watt-hours (Wh) (specifically including the VoltVibe Titan PowerStation and expansion battery blocks) are categorized as Class 9 Dangerous Goods under international maritime and aviation laws.

5. Specialized Lithium-Ion HAZMAT Rules
VoltVibe manufactures high-capacity energy storage products, including the VoltVibe Titan PowerStation and associated modular battery units. Because these items contain high-capacity lithium-ion battery packs exceeding 100 Watt-hours (Wh), their transport is strictly regulated under federal and international hazardous materials (HAZMAT) regulations. Consequently, unique reporting and safety inspection requirements apply to any transit claims involving these products.

5. Specialized Lithium-Ion HAZMAT Rules
CRITICAL RULE: Due to the safety risks associated with thermal runaway in damaged high-capacity battery units, any claim involving a shipment containing a lithium-ion battery pack with a capacity exceeding 100Wh (specifically including the VoltVibe Titan PowerStation and VoltVibe Ranger expansion packs) must be reported to the VoltVibe Safety and Logistics Compliance desk within 48 hours of carrier delivery. The customer must take high-resolution photographs of the outer packaging and the device's housing to verify that no puncture or swelling of the battery cells has occurred. Failure to report damage within this 48-hour window voids all transit insurance coverage, and the product will be treated as accepted in as-is condition. Additionally, damaged high-capacity batteries cannot be returned via standard carrier networks; they must be disposed of at an authorized local HAZMAT recycling center, and the customer must upload a signed certificate of disposal to complete their claim.

While transit insurance is generally applicable worldwide, specific geographic regions are excluded from coverage due to high historical rates of loss, customs impoundments, or courier infrastructure deficits. If a customer requests a specific carrier at checkout, any additional shipping surcharges will be billed directly to the
customer and are non-refundable, even if a transit claim is subsequently approved.

3. Domestic vs. International Processing Pathways
Transit claims are routed through two separate administrative tracks. Domestic claims (within the US and Canada) are processed internally by the VoltVibe Claims Desk, with typical resolution timelines ranging between 3 to 5 business days from documentation receipt. In contrast, international claims involving multi-jurisdictional transport require joint investigation with the primary carrier's international liaison. These international claims are subject to a mandatory 30-day monitoring period to allow for customs clearance verification before any payout or replacement can be authorized. If a package is held by international customs authorities due to regulatory non-compliance or unpaid duties (as detailed in doc_07_customs_duties.pdf), the transit insurance is rendered null and void, and the customer must resolve the clearance issue directly with local customs agents.

2. Detailed Quality Grading Criteria Matrix
Refurbished devices are classified into three distinct tiers: Grade A (Like-New), Grade B (Light Wear), and Grade C (Value Grade). Classification is based on physical cosmetic imperfections and current battery health metrics, as summarized in the quality matrix table below:
Quality Grade, 1 = Cosmetic Condition. Quality Grade, 2 = Battery Capacity Packaging & Accessories. Grade A (Like-New), 1 = Zero visible scratches or scuffs on screen/casing. Grade C (Value Grade), 1 = Visible cosmetic wear, small dents on casing corners, minor scuffs. 80% to 84%. Grade C (Value Grade), 2 = Generic brown box. Device only, charger and cable s

3.1 Replaced Battery Module Grading Inconsistency
AMBIGUOUS GRADING EXCLUSION: Devices that undergo complete battery module replacements are subject to unique grading rules. Although a replaced battery has a chemical health metric of 100%, the overall device cannot be graded as Grade A (Like-New) regardless of how pristine the cosmetic casing is. All battery-replaced devices are strictly limited to Grade B or Grade C classification. This policy is in place to account for minor tool marks or seal modifications introduced during the disassembly and replacement process. Conversely, if a device is graded as Grade A, it is guaranteed to contain its original factory battery with a health score of 95% or higher, ensuring the device's water-resistance seals remain completely unbroken since the original factory assembly.

3.2 Salvage Grade / Recyclable Only Threshold
If a device fails to turn on, is blacklisted by cellular carriers (reported stolen/lost), contains a cracked display panel, shows severe screen burn-in (ghosting), or has a non-functional digitizer, it is classified as 'Recyclable Only.' Recyclable devices do not qualify for percentage-based trade-in credits and are awarded a flat $15.00 USD salvage store credit to encourage eco-friendly recycling. The shipping cost to return a rejected salvage device must be covered by the customer if they choose not to recycle it, as detailed in Section 4.2.

1. The Refurbishing & Diagnostic Process Flow
Every pre-owned or customer-returned device received at VoltVibe's centralized processing facilities undergoes a rigorous, multi-stage diagnostic testing flow prior to grading. Devices are initially cleaned, sanitized, and subjected to physical inspection. Once basic telemetry is established, the device is connected to our proprietary hardware diagnostics bench (VibeScan Utility v4.1) for an automated 30-point functionality evaluation. This evaluation verifies logical circuit integrity, baseband connectivity, multi-band Wi-Fi/Bluetooth signal attenuation, ambient light sensors, gyroscope calibration, display digitizer responsiveness, speaker resonance, and microphone distortion. Devices that fail any core operational tests are routed directly to the repair queue, where genuine OEM replacement modules are installed by certified technicians before the device is allowed to re-enter the grading queue. All refurbished devices are guaranteed to have 100% functional equivalence to brand-new units, regardless of their final cosmetic classification.

2. International Shipping Terms (DDU / DAP)
All international shipments are dispatched under Delivery Duty Unpaid (DDU) terms, also known as Delivered at Place (DAP). Under these terms, VoltVibe's shipping carriers (primarily DHL Express and FedEx International) will contact the customer directly once the package arrives at the local border hub. The carrier will provide an itemized invoice detailing the customs clearance fees and local import duties. The carrier will also charge a standardized administrative brokerage fee (typically ranging from $15.00 to $25.00 USD, or a fixed percentage of duties paid, whichever is higher) to cover their border filing expenses. VoltVibe has no control over these brokerage rates and cannot request waivers on behalf of the importer.

3. Border Delay Procedures and Documentation
Customs clearances can cause unpredictable transit delays that are outside the carrier's standard delivery guarantees.

2. Geographic Exclusions and Carrier-Specific Limitations
International transit corridors to Eastern Europe and Central Asia are subject to strict customs verification. If a shipment is seized or delayed by foreign customs due to suspected regulatory non-compliance of the imported goods, the transit insurance policy is immediately suspended. It is the importer's responsibility to verify that the items ordered are compliant with local import restrictions. Furthermore, customs clearance fees, import duties, and VAT are not covered by this policy and must be paid directly to the local taxing authority in accordance with doc_07_customs_duties.pdf.
Carrier-specific limitations apply to remote areas. For example, DHL Express does not support signature-required delivery in certain remote islands. In these cases, if the customer requests signature delivery, VoltVibe will attempt to reroute the package via FedEx. If no signature-capable carrier is available for the destination, the transaction will be canceled and a full refund issued.

3.2 Regional Surcharges and Military Address Exclusions
Shipments delivered to locations outside the contiguous United States (including Alaska, Hawaii, Puerto Rico, Guam, and the US Virgin Islands) are subject to a flat-rate $15.00 USD remote area surcharge, regardless of the package weight tier. Standard shipping to military APO/FPO/DPO addresses is supported, but overnight and express options are blocked due to government security screening requirements. Customers shipping to military locations must select standard shipping at checkout, and transit times can take up to 21 business days.

5.2 Returns, Restocking Fees, and Active Lock Exclusions
Customers may return Grade A or Grade B refurbished devices within 30 days of delivery, subject to a 10% restocking fee (calculated on the final purchase price, excluding taxes and shipping, in accordance with doc_06_restocking_fees.pdf). Grade C devices, promotional items, and outlet clearance units are considered final sale and cannot be returned for refund or credit, except where statutory protections apply (such as in California, Oregon, and the EEA). CRITICAL CRITERIA: Any returned refurbished device must have all security accounts removed and be factory reset. If a device is returned with an active security lock (such as Apple iCloud Activation Lock, Samsung SmartLock, or Google Device Protection), the return will be rejected. The customer will be notified via email and must remove the lock remotely via their cloud portal. If the lock is not removed within 5 business days of notification, the device will be returned to the customer at their expense, and they will be charged a $45 administrative security bypass processing fee.

To ensure proper investigation and verification, claims must be filed within strict reporting windows. Filing windows depend on the category of the claim (Loss in Transit, Damaged Package, or Stolen Package). Failure to submit a claim within the designated period will result in immediate and final rejection of the claim without exception. All claims must be accompanied by the standard documentation package, which includes: the original order confirmation number, carrier tracking details, a signed declaration of non-receipt (for lost/stolen packages), and photographic evidence (for damaged packages).
A package is legally defined as 'Lost in Transit' when carrier tracking records show no scan activity for a prolonged period. The timeline for filing a Lost in Transit claim is conditional on the shipping tier selected at checkout:
· Standard Economy Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 14 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 30 calendar days from the date of shipment.
· Express and Priority Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 7 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 21 calendar days from the date of shipment.
· International Priority Shipping: Claims may only be filed after 21 calendar days of zero scan updates, and no later than 45 calendar days from the date of shipment.

4. Claim Filing Windows and Requirements
For packages marked as 'delivered' by the carrier but arrived damaged, the customer must submit a claim along with photographic evidence within 10 business days of delivery. For packages marked as 'delivered' but physically missing (suspected porch piracy), the standard reporting window is 14 calendar days from the delivery timestamp. However, please note the critical police reporting requirements in Section 6 which mandate actions within 7 calendar days.
All damage claims must submit photos of the outer box, inner packaging materials, and the damaged device's serial number label. If the customer discards the outer packaging before taking photographs, the claim will be immediately denied, as our team cannot verify if the damage occurred during transit or after delivery.

3.1 Loading Dock Load Limits
The loading docks are rated for a maximum static weight load of 15,000 lbs per bay. Forklifts carrying multiple crates of VoltVibe Titan power units (each unit weighing approximately 85 lbs, packaged in wooden shipping crates) must not exceed the maximum dock weight limit. If a delivery vehicle contains a double-stacked pallet of power units, the forklift operator must split the pallet before transporting it across the dock levelers to prevent mechanical stress.

Effective Date: March 2025
This document sets forth standard safety protocols for operating motorized forklifts and warehouse pallet jacks inside the primary VoltVibe Fulfillment Center (Fulfillment Hub A). Operators must hold active certifications and undergo daily safety checks before starting their shifts.
Battery Charging Protocols: Motorized heavy machinery battery swaps must take place exclusively in the designated Battery Charging Zone located on the north wall of Zone 3. The zone must be kept clean, dry, and free of cardboard debris. Forklifts must be fully powered off, and emergency brakes engaged before connecting charging cables. In case of battery acid spills, utilize the nearby acid neutralization spill kits (located at Station 3-B) and notify the warehouse shift manager immediately. Proper protective gloves and face guards are mandatory.

2.1 Heavy Machinery Shutdown Guidelines
Before exiting, forklift operators and heavy machinery users must safely park their vehicles. Forklifts must be parked in their designated charging bays with the forks lowered completely to the floor and the parking brake engaged. This prevents the vehicle from blocking emergency fire doors or hindering emergency vehicle access to the loading docks (in accordance with doc_11_forklift_safety.pdf). Operators must leave their ignition keys in the dashboard in case emergency responders need to move the machinery.

CRITICAL SAFETY RULE: Under standard facility safety protocols (as outlined in
doc_19_evacuation_protocol.pdf), any warehouse worker who detects a Category 2 battery swelling event (characterized by visible smoke or case deformation) must immediately pull the manual fire pull station, and the warehouse supervisor must order a full physical evacuation of the building. However, as a specific operational exception, if the battery swelling event occurs inside the designated Class 9 HAZMAT isolation vault (Bay 4-C), the supervisor should NOT order a building evacuation unless the vault's integrated thermal sensors report a rise in temperature exceeding 3°C per minute. The isolation vault is designed to contain a thermal event for up to 2 hours, and evacuating the entire building prematurely causes significant processing downtime and halts emergency containment controls.
In the event of a vault thermal run, the gaseous clean agent suppression system will activate automatically. Staff must remain outside the vault to allow the Novec gas to isolate and extinguish the event. Entering the vault while the suppression system is active is strictly prohibited without self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA).

1. Purpose and Facility Scope
This document details the internal warehouse operations, safety guidelines, and emergency protocols for handling, packing, and storing high-capacity battery units at VoltVibe's centralized logistics facilities. Given the presence of high-capacity lithium-ion battery modules (specifically the VoltVibe Titan PowerStation and Ranger Expansion modules), all personnel must follow the strict handling procedures detailed in this document. Failure to adhere to these safety regulations is grounds for immediate termination and represents a significant risk of industrial fires.
The warehouse safety guidelines apply to all full-time employees, temporary workers, and third-party logistics contractors entering the active storage and shipping zones. Safety training is mandatory and must be completed prior to operating any heavy machinery or handling inventory. The facility safety coordinator conducts weekly safety drills and inspections to ensure compliance with federal and state environmental safety standards.
In addition to battery-specific protocols, the facility maintains standard safety equipment, including eye-wash stations, safety showers, first-aid kits, and automated external defibrillators (AEDs). All safety equipment locations are marked with highly visible signage and must remain clear of inventory blocks at all times.

8. Additional Support Administration Rules
Support agents must review daily compliance announcements before starting their shift. Any updates to local tax legislation, hazardous materials classifications, or custom duties (governed by doc_07) will be posted in the shared team channel and must be incorporated into customer responses.
If a customer requests details regarding our environmental sustainability or battery disposal program, agents must route them to the public documentation outlined in doc_24_environmental_recycling_policy.pdf. Agents must not provide custom advice regarding environmental certifications or raw metal shredding rates, as these are subject to regular updates by the environmental compliance desk.

Category: Environmental & Sustainability Policies (External) | ID: doc_24_environmental_recycling_policy | Rev: 1.9 Effective Date: January 1, 2026

4.2 Non-Return and Disposal Protocol
Agents must NEVER instruct a customer to mail back a damaged, swollen, or venting battery pack. Under federal regulations, shipping unstable lithium-ion batteries via commercial mail networks is illegal and carries significant fines. Instead, the agent must instruct the customer to place the unit outdoors in a secure, non-flammable area (such as a concrete driveway) away from combustible materials. The customer must be instructed to dispose of the battery at a licensed local hazardous waste center and provide a signed disposal certificate, matching the procedures of doc_21_transit_insurance_claims.pdf. Tier 2 agents are authorized to issue a replacement unit only after the safety desk approves the customer's disposal receipt.

2. The Battery Recycling Rewards Program & Enrollment
Please note that the mail-in recycling program is strictly limited to stable, undamaged battery modules. Under international hazardous cargo shipping laws, mailing damaged, swollen, punctured, or venting battery packs is illegal and carries severe regulatory fines. If a customer possesses an unstable battery module, they must utilize the local physical safety disposal track detailed in Section 5.1, rather than attempting to ship the unit back to VoltVibe.
Furthermore, customers who participate in the recycling program are eligible to receive custom environmental achievement badges on their loyalty profile. These badges grant additional multipliers during specific green promotion periods, allowing customers to earn bonus loyalty points on their hardware purchases.

5. Specialized Lithium-Ion HAZMAT Rules
When a damaged battery is reported, VoltVibe will dispatch an electronic Safety Inspection Form. The customer must complete this form, answering questions regarding potential heat generation, leaking electrolytes, or strange odors. If a battery is determined to be unstable, standard shipping carriers are legally prohibited from transporting it. In such cases, the customer must contact local environmental services for safe disposal. VoltVibe will reimburse the customer for local disposal fees up to $50 USD upon presentation of a valid receipt alongside the HAZMAT certificate of disposal. Any attempt to mail a damaged battery back to VoltVibe without explicit written authorization from our HAZMAT safety director will result in immediate termination of the claim and a permanent ban on the customer's account.
Our safety team coordinates directly with local waste management authorities to verify the authenticity of all submitted disposal certificates. If a certificate is found to be forged or altered, the customer's account will be blacklisted, and the details will be forwarded to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for legal enforcement.
